inspect-unstaged-changes-with-diff.label: Inspect Exact Project Changes
inspect-unstaged-changes-with-diff.summary: Inspect exact supplied text changes without requiring knowledge of any programming language.

sketch2.title: Name the File When Focus Matters

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: Change and Inspect the Cleanup Time
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt: Open `community-project/schedule.txt`. Find the exact line `Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM` and replace it with `Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM`. Save the file and change nothing else. Then inspect only the unstaged change in `schedule.txt`.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Make the supplied one-line edit, then review that file before staging.

help.concept: git diff compares the working-tree file with its staged version; the exact edit is supplied because this course assesses Git, not the file format.
help.hint_1: Do not change the title or any other line.
help.hint_2: Inspect the saved change before staging it.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C community-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Old planning notes

Spring flyer draft.
' > community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s 'Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.
' > community-project/generated/summary.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Start community project'

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you inspect only the unstaged schedule change after saving the exact edit?

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: Change and Inspect the Help Desk Number
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt: Keep the schedule change. Open `community-project/contacts.txt`. Find `Help desk: 555-0100` and replace it with `Help desk: 555-0130`. Save the file and change nothing else. Then inspect only the unstaged change in `contacts.txt`.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Preserve the earlier schedule edit while reviewing the new contact edit separately.

help.concept: A path-limited diff focuses on one change while another unfinished file remains in the same working tree.
help.hint_1: Use the exact supplied text replacement; no outside file-format knowledge is needed.
help.hint_2: The schedule.txt change must remain present.

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: Focus the Diff on One Changed File
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt: Both supplied edits now exist. Inspect only `schedule.txt` and confirm that the output shows the cleanup-time change while leaving both files untouched.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Limit the comparison to the schedule path; inspection must not alter either edit.

help.concept: Path-limited inspection does not stage, restore, or modify either file.
help.hint_1: Only schedule.txt should appear in this diff.
help.hint_2: contacts.txt remains changed for the next topic.

single-choice-1.title: Using git diff
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: After the change has already been committed
options.c: When you want to list earlier commit subjects
options.d: When you want to create repository metadata

multi-choice-2.title: Interpreting Diff Output
multi-choice-2.prompt: Which symbol in `git diff` output indicates a line has been added?
multi-choice-2.hint: Choose the one answer that describes this command or concept precisely.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Remove choices that act on a different repository layer or promise behavior Git does not provide.
help.hint_2: Prefer the exact definition used in this chapter over a nearby Git idea.

fill-blank-choice-1.title: Symbol for Removed Lines
fill-blank-choice-1.prompt: In `git diff`, lines that have been removed are indicated by the symbol `[blank1]`.
fill-blank-choice-1.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

fill-blank-choice-1.template: In `git diff`, lines that have been removed are indicated by the symbol `[blank1]`.

fill-blank-choice-2.title: Purpose of git diff
fill-blank-choice-2.prompt: The primary purpose of `git diff` is to show changes in the [blank1] that are not yet staged.
fill-blank-choice-2.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

fill-blank-choice-2.template: The primary purpose of `git diff` is to show changes in the [blank1] that are not yet staged.

sketch0.title: Diff Shows Exact Text Changes
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: `git diff -- path` compares a working-tree file with its staged version. Removed lines begin with `-`; added lines begin with `+`. Because every edit is supplied exactly, your job is to inspect the Git change rather than invent file content.

sketch2.title: Name the File When Focus Matters
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: When several text files differ, adding `-- path` narrows the comparison to one file. This keeps the question focused and does not alter repository state.

sketch3.title: Inspect Before You Stage
sketch3.bodyMarkdown: A diff is the last review point before an edit enters the proposed snapshot. Later lessons reuse this habit in new decisions without reteaching the command from the beginning.
